focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: At Rudy's bar on New Year's Eve, Kath breaks up with Carl, frustrated by his emotional detachment, but Carl calmly accepts, leading to a brief hug as she cries.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl is broken up with by Kath, who criticizes his emotional detachment and lack of ambition; he responds with calm acceptance rather than emotional reaction.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally withdrawn, relationally unresponsive
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: lack of emotional expression may hinder future connections
evidence_quotes: KATH You don't do anything more. You used to be fun. Now you hardly ever even leave your apartment.
CARL (ACQUIESCING) Well, look, this sucks, but I understand where you're coming from.
KATH This is the problem. You don't even care that I'm breaking up with you.
CARL We're at different places in our lives. I get it.

relation_updates: romantic relationship with Kath ends
status_updates: emotionally detached as perceived by others

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl, a teller, jealously watches Chris Parker, the new bank president, who arrives with his secretary; Carl redirects a customer to Manager Norman, expressing envy over Chris's success and lifestyle.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl observes Chris Parker's arrival as the new bank president and reacts with visible jealousy, highlighting his discontent with his own static role.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl remains in his position as a teller, emotionally affected by the contrast between his stagnant job and Chris Parker's success.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl shows no immediate steps to improve his situation despite his evident dissatisfaction.
evidence_quotes: Carl stares at them, jealously.
CARL I'm Carl Kendall. Just a teller.
Yeah, if you're into fake breasts and German engineering.
Carl slowly moves so a column blocks the customer's view of him.

relation_updates: develops negative comparison toward Chris Parker
status_updates: acknowledges class/status gap in workplace hierarchy

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl, overwhelmed by the realization of a decade spent in stagnation, resists Peter's confrontation, clings to autonomy, and retreats into isolation, hinting at a potential turning point with the 'Just Say Yes' pamphlet.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After Peter confronts him about his isolated lifestyle, Carl resists but is shaken by the realization that he has spent ten years as a bank teller with no personal progress, leading to visible distress and a moment of quiet reflection when he uncrumples the 'Just Say Yes' pamphlet.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: defensive about his choices and resistant to outside judgment
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally unsettled and momentarily self-aware, showing a break in his usual detachment
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to respond to the realization that his life has not moved forward
evidence_quotes: CARL (SHOCKED) I've been a teller at Southwest Bank for ten years? How the hell did that happen?
Ten years... if I had had a child, he'd be toilet-trained by now.
Carl pushes Peter out the door.
He then unballs the \"Just Say Yes\" pamphlet.

relation_updates: strained relationship with Peter due to social withdrawal
status_updates: acknowledges a decade of personal stagnation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sanji challenges Carl to say yes for a day, urging him to embrace change; Carl agrees, sparking celebration and marking a turning point in his journey toward overcoming regret and hesitation.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: responding to a personal challenge from Sanji
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl agrees to Sanji's request to say yes to everything for one day, marking a shift from resistance to tentative engagement with change.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: commit to saying yes for one day
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: publicly committed and emotionally affected by the moment
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether this one-day experiment will lead to lasting change
evidence_quotes: I want you only to say yes. Can you do that for me? For one day, Carl?
I'm not sure --
Please, Carl. Do it for me and you will be doing it for yourself.
Yes?

relation_updates: develops moment of intimacy with Sanji
status_updates: publicly commits to personal transformation
auxiliary.persona_anchor: adoption of 'yes' as a temporary behavioral rule

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl secretly uses Norman's loan stamp to approve a $5k loan for Marge's beanie baby business after lying about bank policy, accepting a panda beanie as thanks.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl takes Norman's loan approval stamp without permission and uses it to approve a $5,000 loan for Marge, violating bank policy.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: help customer despite policy
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: complicit in unauthorized bank action
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: risk of being caught using the stamp without authorization
evidence_quotes: Carl heads back over to Norman's desk and SWIPES THE LOAN APPROVAL STAMP.
Carl returns to the loan desk and STAMPS HER FORM.
CARL: Of course we can. One moment please.
The Bank Customer HUGS Carl.

relation_updates: forms trusting bond with customer Marge
status_updates: in possession of a panda beanie baby
acts beyond teller role

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Norman offers Carl a permanent loan specialist role at $52,000, celebrates with a Heroes-themed party next Saturday, and Carl returns to work happy about his promotion.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl accepts a permanent position as a loan specialist with a salary of $52,000, transitioning from his previous role as a teller and greeter.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: accepts promotion and agrees to attend celebration party
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: has accepted the new role and returns to work in a positive emotional state
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how he will perform in the new role
evidence_quotes: What do you say to a permanent spot at the loan desk?
We need a loan specialist a lot more than we need a teller and greeter. Besides, you'd have a starting salary of 52.
CARL Grand?
(then, back to Carl) Of course grand.

relation_updates: improved rapport with Norman Calhoun
Norman shares personal interest in Heroes
status_updates: promoted from teller/greeter to loan specialist
salary increased to $52,000
auxiliary.persona_anchor: first tangible sign of upward mobility

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl agrees to a binding 'Maniyesto' requiring him to say yes to all requests until New Year's, under strict rules, and is branded with 'YES' on his palm by Sanji.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl agrees to the Maniyesto, committing to say yes to all requests until New Year's, and is physically branded on the palm with 'YES' by Sanji.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Commit to living without refusal for a defined period to fully engage with life experiences.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl is now formally bound by the rules of the Maniyesto, required to accept all requests and maintain constant availability, marked by a physical brand on his palm.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The psychological and practical effects of enforced compliance remain uncertain.
evidence_quotes: I say... yes.
Carl signs the Maniyesto.
Sanji PULLS OUT an iron brand that's been heating over a hot pot and BRANDS CARL'S PALM.
In the center of the palm, Sanji's branded a `YES.'

relation_updates: enters adversarial yet transformative relationship with Sanji
status_updates: becomes a designated 'Yes Man'
physically marked with 'YES' brand on palm

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl Kendall is praised by President Chris Parker for successful microloan results, leading to an invitation on a corporate golf trip, and is seen as a rising talent.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl is commended by the bank president for increasing customer traffic and launching a profitable microloan initiative, leading to an invitation on a corporate golf trip.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: gain recognition and advancement through community-focused banking
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl is acknowledged as a high-potential employee and invited into executive circles, though viewed with cautious skepticism.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: ambiguity around whether Carl's motives are altruistic or strategically self-serving.
evidence_quotes: You've upped walk-in traffic by 35%.
You've given out over 682 below minimum loans. One of them was literally for fifteen dollars and eighty seven cents.
You're the kind of person we want in corporate. We're taking some clients on a golfing trip. You golf?

relation_updates: Chris Parker views Carl as a rising talent
status_updates: Carl transitions from low-level employee to potential corporate successor
auxiliary.persona_anchor: tension between being a team player and an ambitious climber

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl's habit of agreeing to everything causes Renee to question his sincerity, leading her to leave when she realizes his responses lack genuine choice or feeling.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Renee ends the relationship after realizing Carl's automatic agreement to everything, including her emotional question about falling for her, makes his responses feel insincere and unauthentic.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to convince Renee he is sincere
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: romantic relationship terminated
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl's inability to express genuine preferences
evidence_quotes: RENEE So you've just been saying yes to everything.
CARL Yes. (THEN) Pardon the pun.
RENEE When I asked if you were falling for me all you said was yes.
CARL It wasn't a lie.

relation_updates: Renee emotionally disengages and ends the relationship
status_updates: perceived as emotionally unauthentic

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl realizes Sanji is a con artist after being misled, panics about his branded hand, and exposes him before fleeing the restaurant.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl realizes Sanji is exploiting him when Sanji tries to sell him an expensive seminar instead of offering genuine advice, triggering panic about his branded 'yes' and the life decisions he's made based on Sanji's influence.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeks genuine guidance but is met with exploitation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: disillusioned and desperate
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to reclaim agency after being misled
evidence_quotes: CARL (REALIZING) You're a total con artist.
I've put my life into the hands of a con artist! And because of you I have this retarded yes branded on my hand for life!
This man is a con artist! Don't listen to this man! He is a con artist!
Carl runs out of the restaurant.

relation_updates: breaks trust with Sanji
publicly confronts Sanji as a fraud
status_updates: experiences emotional panic
loses faith in the 'yes' philosophy

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl's sabotage of the merger through offensive fake translation is exposed by Seung Kim, who speaks fluent English, leading to the deal's collapse and Carl's resignation.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl deliberately provides false and offensive translations during the merger announcement, which are exposed by Seung Kim, leading Carl to resign on the spot.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl is no longer employed at Southwest Bank, and the merger with Korean Bank International is terminated due to the incident.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl's reasons for sabotaging the merger are not fully explained, and his future plans are unclear.
evidence_quotes: CARL (in Korean, with SUBTITLES) [He says that you are a son of a dog and like to have the dirty sex.]
CARL (in Korean, with SUBTITLES) [He says in Korea you poop on the ground and that here you cannot poop on the ground.]
CARL (in Korean) [Although you have poop on your hand, I am willing to shake it so I can steal all your money, you stupid Korean.]
CARL I quit.

relation_updates: relationship with Seung Kim turns from false respect to open hostility
trust with Chris Parker collapses
status_updates: resigns from job
merger fails due to his actions
auxiliary.persona_anchor: act of self-sabotage resulting in professional downfall

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl and Renee visit Omar, the former King of Nigeria, in Makurdi, where they are welcomed warmly and invited to a feast at his palace.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl travels to Nigeria to meet Omar in person, apologizes for a past insult, and is invited to a feast at his palace.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl shifts from conducting remote microloans to engaging directly with borrowers and community leaders in person.
evidence_quotes: CARL I'm sorry for calling you a masturbatory Russian teenager.
OMAR, FORMER KING OF NIGERIA Water under the bridge. What do you say to having a feast?
Carl and Renee get out a dusty minivan. A dirty, rundown palace rises before them.
CARL I'm here to see Omar. The former King of Nigeria.

relation_updates: reconciliation with Omar
establishes personal connection with former King of Nigeria
status_updates: present in Makurdi, Nigeria
engaged in international microloan work

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Carl Kendall begins as an emotionally detached bank teller stuck in a monotonous routine, marked by social withdrawal and professional stagnation. A series of events, catalyzed by a self-improvement seminar, push him into a transformative journey where he adopts a 'say yes' philosophy, leading to impulsive decisions, professional advancement, and strained personal relationships. As his compulsive compliance leads to crisis—including arrest and betrayal—he ultimately rejects corporate ambition, resigns in disgrace, and departs for a new life rooted in authentic human connection and global microloan work.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl, overwhelmed by the realization of a decade spent in stagnation, resists Peter's confrontation, clings to autonomy, and retreats into isolation, hinting at a potential turning point with the 'Just Say Yes' pamphlet.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: concerned friend confronting Carl about his reclusive lifestyle
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Peter directly confronts Carl about his social withdrawal, missed events, and emotional stagnation, framing the visit as an intervention.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Peter wants Carl to re-engage with social life and attend important events like the engagement party.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Peter has escalated from passive concern to direct confrontation, but is physically pushed out by Carl, leaving his efforts unsuccessful in the moment.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl rejects Peter's intervention and insists on living independently, maintaining his isolation.
evidence_quotes: This is an intervention. You've missed your last event.
You're watching Hell's Kitchen. That's not even B-list reality TV.
It's bigger than that. You and Kath broke up over a year ago and you still aren't dating anyone.
You've become a complete cave dweller.

relation_updates: asserts concern for Carl's emotional and social well-being
shifts from passive observer to active intervener in Carl's life
status_updates: takes on role of catalyst for change
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Peter as a socially engaged friend who challenges stagnation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl reluctantly agrees to join Peter, Lucy, and Kath for brunch despite a mental image of Sanji urging him, conflicting with his initial hesitation.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: response to social invitation with internal conflict
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Peter calls Carl to apologize and invite him to brunch, leading Carl to reluctantly agree despite hesitation influenced by his internal struggle.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: reestablish connection with Carl after prior confrontation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl accepts the invitation to brunch, indicating a temporary reconnection with the group.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl's reluctance and internal conflict suggest uncertainty about the durability of the reconciliation.
evidence_quotes: PETER (V.O.) (on the phone) Carl, it's Peter. I just wanted to apologize about barging in yesterday. You were right. It's your life to live.
PETER (V.O.) (on the phone) Me, Lucy and Kath are all going out for brunch. You have any interest in joining?
CARL Oh, thanks for the invite, but...
CARL (in slo-mo) Yes.

relation_updates: reaffirms connection with Carl after prior conflict
status_updates: shifts from confrontation to reconciliation
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Peter as someone seeking to maintain group cohesion through direct apology and inclusion

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl confronts the waitress about a line-cutting group, but a diner admits they were wrong, apologizes, and reconciles with Carl, resolving the tension peacefully.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: observer and moderator during Carl's confrontation with the waitress
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Peter questions Carl's aggressive response to the seating mix-up and physically intervenes to de-escalate the situation.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: maintain group cohesion during public conflict
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Peter distances himself from Carl's behavior and aligns with the group's preference for calm resolution.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Peter will consistently challenge Carl's confrontational tendencies in social settings.
evidence_quotes: If you want to be sitting so bad, why don't you say something?
Peter pulls Carl away from the Waitress.
What's going on with you?

relation_updates: tension with Carl over escalation
aligns with group norm of de-escalation
status_updates: emerges as voice of moderation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl, now drunk, pleads with Peter and Lucy not to leave him with Rooney as they depart for furniture errands, but they laugh it off and go, leaving him with Rooney.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Peter laughs off Carl's plea not to be left alone with Rooney, failing to respond to his visible discomfort and intoxication.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Peter leaves Carl in an uncomfortable situation with Rooney, showing disregard for Carl's emotional state.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Peter's tendency to dismiss or overlook emotional cues from others, particularly Carl.
evidence_quotes: CARL (kind of pleading) Please don't leave me here with him.
Peter and Lucy laugh, not realizing that Carl is serious. They leave.

relation_updates: underestimates Carl's emotional dependence
prioritizes convenience over support
status_updates: Carl is visibly intoxicated
Carl is left in an uncomfortable social situation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl, committed to saying 'yes,' agrees to throw Lucy's wedding shower and buy drinks for Rooney and friends despite reluctance, with Peter skeptical and amused by his choices.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Peter agrees to be Carl's 'Yesponsor,' committing to hold him accountable for saying yes to every request for one year.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: support Carl's self-improvement experiment
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Peter is now formally involved in Carl's commitment to say yes, despite expressing doubt about its feasibility.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Peter will intervene if Carl's 'yes' rule leads to harmful decisions.
evidence_quotes: That's where you come in. You're my \"Yesponsor.\"
Like in AA. I need you to hold me to it.
You aren't going to last for more than a week.
Peter and Carl shake hands.

relation_updates: becomes Carl's accountability partner
enters into a structured friendship role
status_updates: Carl adopts a new behavioral rule: say yes to everything
Peter is skeptical but participates in the agreement
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Peter as skeptical but complicit enabler

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl explains his compulsive 'yes' attitude to Peter, justifying his actions including hooking up with a man and attending Korean class despite disliking Korean food, while carrying his adopted kitten.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Peter voices concern as Carl describes adhering to a strict 'yes' rule, leading to actions he personally resists, such as adopting a kitten, attending Korean class, and engaging in a same-sex encounter.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: understand the limits of Carl's commitment to saying yes
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Peter recognizes the growing extremity of Carl's behavior but does not challenge it directly, remaining passively involved.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Peter's hesitation to intervene despite discomfort with Carl's choices.
evidence_quotes: PETER I think you're taking this too far.
CARL I have no choice. I'm a yes man. I must say yes.
PETER You hooked up with a dude?
PETER You hate Korean food.

relation_updates: expresses concern that Carl is going too far
status_updates: Carl has adopted a kitten
Carl has engaged in same-sex encounter due to his rule

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl and Renee leave a dinner with friends; Carl plans to go rollerblading with Ralph, despite Renee's preference to go home, highlighting a disconnect in their desires.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Peter admits he is terrified of marriage and expresses concern that his inability to engage with wedding details might make him a bad husband.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Peter wants to be a good husband but fears he is emotionally or practically inadequate.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Peter begins to acknowledge his fears about marriage rather than dismiss them.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Peter can actively engage in the emotional and practical aspects of the relationship.
evidence_quotes: Honestly, I'm terrified.
But what if I really can't tell the difference between ivory and cream? Does that make me a bad husband?

relation_updates: acknowledges shared fear with Lucy
status_updates: emotional vulnerability revealed

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl regrets letting Renee go and questions his job, while Peter dismisses his concerns, prioritizing a personal call over helping him.

timeline_nodes.salient_development: Peter dismisses Carl's concerns about his relationship and job, shifting focus to wedding flower choices when his phone rings.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Peter maintains emotional distance from Carl's struggles while attending to his own relationship matters.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Peter remains disengaged from Carl's emotional turmoil, redirecting the conversation to his wedding plans.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Peter's avoidance of deep emotional engagement with a friend in crisis.
evidence_quotes: You own an apartment because of that job. People don't generally leave good jobs.

Babe, can you wait a second? (covering the phone) I'm going to be on for a while.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl surprises Peter and Lucy with an impromptu engagement celebration at Rudy's, arriving on a stallion, crowning them, and sparking a dance party.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Peter evolves from a concerned friend pushing Carl toward re-engagement to a passive observer of Carl's radical self-reinvention, ultimately confronting his own emotional avoidance in relationships. His interactions with Carl serve as mirrors for his unresolved fears about commitment and emotional responsibility. As Carl departs, Peter is left with both practical and symbolic custody of his friend's life, marking a quiet but significant shift in his role. The arc traces Peter’s incremental movement from external intervention to internal reckoning.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

acknowledges Carl's crush and redefines him as shy, not threatening
status_updates: exhibiting artist with public gallery presence

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl realizes he enjoys his job at the bank, where he runs a microloan program, surprising himself after years of dismissing it. Renee finds it cool and wants to leave for Norman's theme party, which Carl describes as funny, not crazy.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Renee reveals she has purchased a ticket for a trip around the world in four months and expresses a belief that not everything needs a goal.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to take a trip around the world
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: has committed to a world trip by purchasing a ticket and openly discussing the plan
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: feels terrified about the upcoming journey
evidence_quotes: Besides, I can't open a store. My big master plan is to take a trip around the world.
I bought a ticket for four months from now.
Honestly, I'm terrified.

status_updates: has a ticket for a world trip in four months

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl massages Renee repeatedly over an hour despite hand cramps, continuing even after she falls asleep and asks him to keep going.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Renee requests repeated massages over an extended period, including while asleep, and Carl complies despite physical discomfort.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: receiving physical comfort and attention
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Renee continues to receive massage after multiple requests, with Carl persisting even when she is asleep and he is in pain.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl's physical discomfort is unaddressed as he continues to provide care in response to Renee's requests.
evidence_quotes: You know what I would love? A massage.
Don't stop.
Can you please keep massaging?

relation_updates: establishes pattern of physical dependence on Carl
status_updates: receives prolonged personal care

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl hesitates on an expensive wine choice after Peter leaves; urged by Lucy, he agrees to order two bottles despite discomfort, in a tense, awkward dinner setting.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Renee expresses irritation at Carl's boasting and corrects his casual dismissal of their shared effort in choosing his suit, asserting her presence in the conversation.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: maintaining authenticity in a setting where Carl is performing for others
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Renee pushes back against Carl's self-presentation and affirms her own identity, shifting from observer to active participant in the interaction
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl continues to seek approval from others, while Renee's concerns remain unaddressed
evidence_quotes: As he shows off, Renee looks irritated.
RENEE You think? We went to three different Prada stores to find the right fit.
RENEE It is big. How about you guys?

relation_updates: Renee challenges Carl's performative behavior in front of friends
status_updates: Renee's role shifts from passive guest to active participant in the relationship dynamic

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl and Renee leave a dinner with friends; Carl plans to go rollerblading with Ralph, despite Renee's preference to go home, highlighting a disconnect in their desires.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Renee declines Carl's plan to go rollerblading and suggests going home to be intimate, which Carl agrees to.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: prioritizing emotional and physical closeness over social obligations
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl abandons his plan to go rollerblading with Ralph and agrees to go home with Renee instead.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl's tendency to say 'yes' to others without considering his own or Renee's preferences remains unexamined in the moment.
evidence_quotes: No. It's ten on a Friday night. And that guy's crazy.
Don't you want to go home and make out?
Yes.

relation_updates: Renee successfully redirects Carl's plans toward their relationship
status_updates: Renee gains agency in shaping their shared time

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl and Renee are kissing when Carl checks his watch; Renee notices, stops, and annoyed, tells him to leave.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Renee ends physical intimacy upon noticing Carl checking his watch, expressing irritation.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: disengage from physical intimacy due to perceived inattention
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Renee becomes annoyed and tells Carl to leave after stopping the kiss.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl's divided attention and its impact on their relationship
evidence_quotes: Carl and Renee are making
Renee stops kissing him.
RENEE (ANNOYED) Just go.

relation_updates: growing tension with Carl
status_updates: emotional distance increases

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl's habit of agreeing to everything causes Renee to question his sincerity, leading her to leave when she realizes his responses lack genuine choice or feeling.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Renee realizes Carl’s automatic agreement makes genuine connection impossible, prompting her to end the interaction.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeking genuine emotional connection
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: disillusioned and disengaged
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Carl can ever express real choice or feeling
evidence_quotes: CARL I don't.
CARL (CONFUSED) Yes. And yes.
RENEE When I asked if you were falling for me all you said was yes.
RENEE Yes. It was.

relation_updates: emotional withdrawal from Carl
loss of romantic trust
status_updates: asserting personal boundaries

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl joins Renee on the roof in Bangkok, exchanges words, and they kiss after his long flight.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Renee accepts Carl's presence in Bangkok and consents to a kiss after questioning his motives, indicating a tentative openness to reconnect.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: reassessing Carl's sincerity and their potential future
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: cautiously hopeful
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Carl’s change is lasting or just another performance
evidence_quotes: RENEE What're you doing here?
RENEE Are you here because you want to be here?
CARL I just flew twenty-two hours. What do you think? Now can I please kiss you?
Renee nods. They kiss.

relation_updates: renewed romantic connection
tentative reconciliation
status_updates: open to emotional risk again

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Renee evolves from a persistent street promoter into a romantically and artistically engaged individual whose relationship with Carl reveals tensions between spontaneity and authenticity. Her journey traces growing emotional investment, increasing awareness of Carl's compulsive people-pleasing, and a crisis of trust after his arrest. Despite disillusionment, she remains open to reconciliation, ultimately reengaging with cautious hope after Carl seeks her out across distances.
